mappings.rationale: A core text on philosophies of education, teaching methods, educational systems, and the organization of schooling (A, B.2, C).
Examines higher education institutions including colleges, universities, and professional schools (C.1.c).
Explores processes of learning including conditioning, memory, and experimental theories of knowledge acquisition (B.1).
Traces cognitive development, language acquisition, and brain maturation in children, providing essential context for pedagogy and preschool education (B.1, C.1.a).
Addresses the cognitive mechanics of thinking, reasoning, and problem solving that underpin the learning process (B.1).
Examines theories of intelligence and IQ testing, with implications for understanding learning processes and special education (B.1, C.1.d).
Essential for understanding how memory processes enable learning and the retention of knowledge (B.1).
Explores creative thinking as an integral dimension of the learning process and educational development (B.1).
Provides the psychological foundations of learning, cognition, and developmental psychology that inform educational theory (B.1).
Adolescent development is central to secondary education, addressing the unique cognitive and social needs of teenage learners (C.1.b).
Uta Frith's pioneering account of autism provides critical insight into special education and the education of exceptional children (C.1.d).
Addresses dyslexia as a reading difficulty with direct implications for special education practice and intervention strategies (C.1.d).
Reading and literacy are fundamental educational skills; this text examines their role as instructional components (B.2.a).
Explores the psychological dimensions of language processing, directly informing how communication is taught and learned (B.2.a).
AI applications are reshaping computer-based instruction and instructional media in education (B.2.d).
Information theory and communication are foundational to instructional media and educational technology (B.2.d).
Addresses employee training, workplace development, and vocational training programs as forms of continuing education (C.1.f).
Traces historical attitudes toward childhood education and the evolving understanding of children's development (C.1.a, C.1.b).
Human capital theory, a central topic here, illuminates the economics of education and its societal returns (C.3).
